title: ssdsims Development Guide
text: Style: Tidyverse style guide. Use air format committing (configured air.toml). Linting: Run air format apply formatters. Type checks: explicit type hints; rely chk::*() runtime validation function bodies. Validation: Use chk input validation; keep error messages informative actionable. Error origin: Raise errors context user-facing function. Thread public function’s frame validators (chk::abort_chk(..., call = call) call = environment() captured exported function) avoid leaking internal frames like purrr::map() / lapply() / private helpers Error ...: header (loop instead purrr::walk/chk_all surface). package-wide pass enforce tracked error-call-origin roadmap item (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §12). Minimal diffs: touch lines change requires; don’t reformat unrelated lines let editors rewrite whitespace. Leave DESCRIPTION formatting usethis/desc (e.g. keep trailing space field names like Imports:). Orthography: UK/AU (Commonwealth) English — prefer -ise/-isation (initialise, serialisable) -ize, -(behaviour, colour) -. matches package’s R source OpenSpec artifacts authors’ Australian/Canadian convention. Exceptions: code identifiers base R/API names keep canonical spelling (e.g. color, center, RNGkind), proper nouns (e.g. “Apache License”). Example:
code: chk::chk_whole_number(seed) chk::chk_range(nrow, c(5, 1000)) chk::chk_character(dataset_names)

title: ssdsims Development Guide
text: package uses two RNG paths: L’Ecuyer-CMRG (legacy, removed) — with_lecuyer_cmrg_seed(), local_lecuyer_cmrg_state(). dqrng + hash (new targets-based path) — dqrng::dqset.seed(seed, stream) task-derived primers. pcg64 backend scenario-scoped via local_dqrng_backend(), reentrant: nested call -op (detected via RNGkind()[1] == "user-supplied"), outermost scope activates resets backend RNG stream identical without nesting. See R/dqrng-backend.R openspec/changes/dqrng-init/design.md. touching RNG-consuming code: - assume fixed global .Random.seed across function calls. - Use local_lecuyer_cmrg_seed() / local_lecuyer_cmrg_state() / local_dqrng_backend() / withr::local_seed() scope RNG changes. - exit, RNG state must restored (use .exit() withr scoping helpers). - Test .Random.seed unchanged (see tests/testthat/test-lecuyer-cmrg-seed.R examples).

title: ssdsims Development Guide
text: package transitioning immediate ssd_run_scenario() execution cluster-based targets pipeline. Key shifts: Scenario object (ssd_define_scenario()) — Declarative, construction-time, contains seed, knobs, dataset names, arg grids. Per-task RNG — task gets primer derived rlang::hash(task_params), seeded via dqrng::dqset.seed(seed, stream = primer). Shards — Parquet files grouped partition_by axes, stored Hive-partitioned directory tree. Static branching (default) — tar_map() mints one named target per shard sourcing time. Dynamic branching (escape hatch) — extreme fan-outs, task tables can computed inside targets instead. roadmap (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §12) lands features dependency order, starting ssd-define-scenario dqrng-init (dependencies). step coherent working state; parallel work streams encouraged.

text: Terminology used throughout ssdsims.

title: ssdsims Glossary
text: seed: scalar integer passed base::set.seed() (seed argument dqrng::dqset.seed()). base R dqrng accept single integer seed. state: full internal state RNG. L’Ecuyer-CMRG, state length-7 integer vector assignable .Random.seed (passed set.seed()). dqrng, state opaque user code accessible via dqrng::dqrng_get_state() / dqrng_set_state(). ssdsims function names ending _state (e.g. with_lecuyer_cmrg_state, slice_sample_state, fit_dists_state, hc_state) take state argument , new design, holds primer (see ) — function installs running RNG state executing body. stream: independent sequence pseudo-random numbers within RNG family. L’Ecuyer-CMRG, streams advanced via parallel::nextRNGStream() (~2^127 jump per stream); L’Ecuyer stream selector state vector. dqrng, stream separate argument dqset.seed() (independent seed); seed different stream give statistically independent sequences. sub-stream: finer subdivision within L’Ecuyer-CMRG stream, advanced via parallel::nextRNGSubStream(). ssdsims’s current package convention assigns one sub-stream per simulation index (sim). dqrng-based design (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §2) use sub-streams; task gets dqrng stream selected primer. dqrng PCG64: 64-bit integer packed length-2 integer vector (hi32, lo32). primer value passed stream argument dqrng::dqset.seed(). L’Ecuyer-CMRG: length-7 integer state vector assignable .Random.seed. TARGETS-DESIGN.md, per-task primer 64-bit rlang::hash() task’s parameters via task_primer(p) (§2). state = argument _state functions primer task. (historical misnomer, fixed future iteration: primer = argument _primer functions.)

title: ssdsims Glossary
text: task: One row step’s task table (data_tasks / fit_tasks / hc_tasks). task specifies smallest unit computation pipeline: carries per-task primer (§2), parameter values cross-join axes step, upstream partition path depends . Many tasks bundle one shard () share step’s partition_by column values. partition: Hive directory level keyed axis value (e.g. dataset=boron/sim=1/). Hive-partitioned layout read-side concept — query engines (duckplyr / DuckDB) inspect directory names skip files whose path doesn’t match filter (predicate pushdown). scenario’s partition_by[[step]] picks task-table columns become partition levels step (§5). shard: One Parquet file disk — write-side concept. shard unit dispatch (one branch produces one shard), atomic rewrite (one Parquet rewritten task set changes, §8), caching (file existence partition path = cache hit). One shard contains 1+ task results, one row per task, task_id column. design one shard ≡ one partition leaf (part-N style splitting); leaf file always named part.parquet. step: One three RNG-touching stages pipeline: data (slice_sample_state()), fit (fit_dists_state()), hc (hc_state). step task table (data_tasks / fit_tasks / hc_tasks), grid, partition_by axes, dynamic-branched target (data_step / fit_step / hc_step), shard directory. word “step” reserved three stages. target: targets::tar_target() declaration _targets.R script. static target produces one object; dynamic-branched target pattern = map(grouped_tbl) produces one branch per group upstream grouped table. branch: One sub-target dynamic-branched target — produced pattern = map(...) iterated grouped task table. 1 branch = 1 group = 1 shard ; branch body loops K tasks group, primes RNG per task, writes one Parquet. Independent branches run parallel — unit parallelism design commits . branches packed Slurm jobs crew_controller_slurm() open question (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §11). job: Reserved exclusively cluster-scheduler term — Slurm (equivalent) work unit dispatched crew controller. job may host one branch several; precise branch ↔︎ job mapping crew_controller_slurm() open question (TARGETS-DESIGN.md §11). fixed independent shards run parallel — shard granularity unit parallelism, regardless branches packed jobs. Branches shards exist without scheduler; jobs exist cluster.

title: ssdsims Glossary
text: sim: index simulation replicate. nsim: number simulation replicates perform. nrow: number rows (species) simulated dataset. replace: Whether resampling generates simulated data performed replacement.

title: ssdsims Glossary
text: SSD: Species sensitivity distribution: distribution species-level toxicity endpoints used ecological risk assessment. dists: parametric distributions fit SSD data (e.g. lnorm, gamma, llogis); see ssdtools::ssd_fit_dists(). fits: fitdists object holding one fitted distributions. hc: Hazard concentration: quantile SSD given proportion (e.g. hc5 5% quantile); see ssdtools::ssd_hc(). proportion: proportion species affected hazard concentration computed. ci: Whether compute confidence intervals hazard concentrations. ci_method: method used compute confidence intervals (e.g. multi_fixed, weighted_samples). nboot: number bootstrap replicates used computing confidence intervals.

what: 3. The sim column identity claim — confirmed with a caveat
title: RNG flow in ssdsims — from scripts/example.R
text: Claim (user’s question): sim column every row nested tibble identifies unique L’Ecuyer-CMRG substream, fixed (seed, stream, sim) state used ssd_sim_data(), fit_dists_seed() hc_seed(). Verdict — design intent: confirmed. three call sites pass (seed, stream, sim) triple get_lecuyer_cmrg_stream_state{,s}(), helper pure function three inputs. Empirical check (Rscript current main, seed = 42): substream lattice exactly canonical L’Ecuyer-CMRG one: Caveat — implementation bug uncovered verifying. check states_data[[1]] == fit_seed(sim=1) initially came back FALSE fresh R session. Root cause: get_lecuyer_cmrg_state() (R/lecuyer-cmrg-seed.R:156) calls RNGkind("L'Ecuyer-CMRG", …), mutates global RNG kind. get_lecuyer_cmrg_stream_states() wraps .exit(set_state(ostate)), .Random.seed exist call (get_state() returns NULL), set_state(NULL) documented -op — RNG kind restored. next call therefore enters kind already L’Ecuyer-CMRG, set.seed(seed) produces different starting state Mersenne-Twister. inputs, different output. normal usage practical impact small — anything session touched RNG, .Random.seed exists .exit restores properly — real reproducible clean R --vanilla. what: 4. Streams — concept vs. usage in ssdsims
title: RNG flow in ssdsims — from scripts/example.R
text: L’Ecuyer-CMRG defines two levels advancement: parallel::nextRNGStream(s) — jumps ~2^127 draws ahead; canonical way give different parallel workers statistically independent sequences. parallel::nextRNGSubStream(s) — jumps smaller (~2^76) amount; intended separate replicates within worker. ssdsims maps : stream concept used parallelism inside ssdsims? . parallel/furrr/mirai call package. Every call ssd_sim_data() / ssd_fit_dists_sims() / ssd_hc_sims() uses single scalar stream (default 1L, overridable via ssdsims.stream option explicit argument). package iterates purrr::pmap(), sequentially. stream actually , ? user-facing extensibility hook. calling user gets two batches whose substreams overlap (separated nextRNGStream, .e. ~2^127 draws), batches reproducible master seed. words, stream existing “want add sims without disturbing ones already ” axis — exactly lever user’s goal #3 (extensibility) wants push . just hasn’t wired workflow yet.

title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: End--end workflow running ssdsims scenario cluster. Three primary goals, hard constraint: Reproducibility. scenario’s results bit-stable across reruns machines (RNG state explicit serializable; §1, §2). Debuggability. single failed task cluster must replayable locally — targets, orchestrator — using inputs cluster used. _state primitives (slice_sample_state(), fit_dists_state(), hc_state(), taking per-task primer) per-shard Parquet partitions manifest (§7). Extensibility. Shards cache unit: larger scenario reuses shards Parquets already computed computes rest. Path-axis growth adds new shards; inner-axis growth rewrites affected shards atomically (§8). Pinning outputs despite code change stays inside one project via tar_cue(depend = FALSE) — second project needed (§8.3). Parallelism assumed throughout. document covers, order: scenario object central registries (§1), per-task dqrng+hash RNG mechanism (§2), running locally (§3), assembling cluster pipeline (§4), three step grids fan-outs (§5), concrete target graph cloud upload hook (§6), debugging cluster failure (§7), extension (§8). §9 lists known limitations, §10 maps gaps RNG-FLOW.md §5 resolutions, §11 collects open questions, §12 implementation roadmap. Terminology (per GLOSSARY.md): task one row {data,fit,hc}_tasks table — smallest unit computation, carrying primer; shard Parquet file produced running one several tasks (depending partitioning). Step = one three stages data / fit / hc; target = tar_target() declaration; job = Slurm work unit. shard named *_step target (minted tar_map construction time — static branching, §6); shard’s tasks run inside one target, writes 1 shard. Independent shard targets run parallel; map Slurm jobs crew_controller_slurm() open question (§11). Background list gaps design closes RNG-FLOW.md §5. forward-looking design; document existing PoC (PR #59).

Scenario purely declarative (§1). Stores seed, knobs, names datasets min_pmix entries; values resolved implicit registry scenario (§1.1). Names enter per-task hash, function definitions (code edit / JIT move tasks across primers, data stored Parquet files remains simple). Three step grids, one primer per task (§5). Data, fit hc fan independently; nrow never axis data step — every nrow value head(., n) single n_max-row sample. ci = FALSE collapses bootstrap knobs (§1.2). ci = FALSE nboot / ci_method / parametric axes stored NA hc-task table — phantom branches. Static branching — one named target per shard (§6). shard set pure function scenario, known _targets.R sourced, tarchetypes::tar_map() mints one named target per shard (pattern = map runtime task-table target). scenario plain construction-time object, tar_target(). Dynamic branching stays available documented fallback extreme-scale fan-outs (§6). Per-shard Parquet shards, Hive-style (§6). shard target writes one Parquet file results/<step>/dataset=.../sim=.../; downstream shards open upstream shard partition path (format = "file"), duckplyr predicate-pushes filters partition columns. Cloud upload separate target (§6.1). scenario$upload set, upload_<step> target sits downstream shard file pushes Azure Blob (another object store). format = "file" target, content-hashing skips re-uploading shards change; ssd_test_upload() probes backend pipeline init, graph still builds dry-runs credentials. One bad shard abort run (§6.2). Step targets carry error = "null": branch errors goes NULL, targets records error, pipeline keeps building summary unions survivors. Errors read tar_meta() tar_make() returns (target call mid-run). Debug = task row + one upstream shard (§7). failed branch replays locally local_dqrng_state(seed, primer) + immediate-upstream shard, targets needed; lightweight recipe verifies upstream manifest$completed_shards (sha256) running failing step. Extension mostly implicit (§8). Shards cache: larger scenario reuses shards whose partition path already Parquet, adds shards (path-axis growth, §8.1) atomically rewrites (inner-axis growth, §8.2) needed. Pinning shards despite code change (opposite invalidation) tar_cue(depend = FALSE) step targets, one project — child project, parent reference (§8.3). Roadmap parallel work streams (§12). Eighteen kebab-slugged steps Mermaid DAG showing branches open. Two ground-entries — ssd-define-scenario task-list-loop-baseline runner — land RNG / dqrng machinery data shape settled first.

what: Tasks bundle into shards by partition
title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: Tasks unit computation (one row, one primer, one _state call). Shards unit storage, dispatch parallelism: one shard ≡ one Parquet file ≡ one named step target (minted tar_map, §6), independent shards run parallel. (shard targets packed Slurm jobs crew_controller_slurm() open question — see §11.) One shard typically contains many tasks: scenario’s partition_by[[step]] picks task-table columns become Hive directory levels, every task row sharing column values goes shard. Default partition_by: partition_by scenario-configurable; pushing axes path produces smaller, finer-grained shards; pushing fewer axes produces coarser shards. changes: Shard count Π |path axis| per step, Π |task axis|. §6 example (1 dataset · 2 sim · 2 nrow · 2 rescale · 2 est_method, default partition_by): 2 data shards, 4 fit shards, 2 hc shards (instead 4 / 8 / 16 tasks--shards). Per-task RNG unchanged. task shard runs primer; branch body loops shard’s task rows primes RNG per task. Cache granularity per shard, per task. Adding new inner-axis value (e.g. new min_pmix) changes task-row set every affected shard forces atomic rewrite Parquets (existing branches re-run). Adding new path-axis value (e.g. new dataset) creates new shards leaves existing shards untouched — see §8. Static default; dynamic documented escape hatch extreme fan-outs. link steps upstream partition path: shard row carries partition column values upstream shard needs, body opens one upstream Parquet. static branching shard target can additionally name specific upstream shard target precise dependency edge; partition path remains portable contract used -cluster query layer (Hive-style layout ) replay (§7). lets tweaking rescale re-run fit + hc without re-running data (fit shard’s data partition columns unchanged). _targets.R sketch: summary reads three result directories directly duckplyr rather depending shard target — sees whatever shards landed (survivors included, §6.2) pull every shard’s value back R. tarchetypes::tar_combine() alternative fan--memory bind wanted instead. step body (sketch): body loops per task shard, primes RNG task’s primer, calls (state-less) _state primitive, stacks K result rows one Parquet shard’s partition path. fit shard opens one upstream data shard via partition path, fit shard never depends whole data step. static branching scoping natural: fit shard target can name single upstream data shard target (data_step_boron_sim1_…) precise edge, adding new data shards mints new targets leaves existing fit shards untouched — directory-hash indirection required avoid spurious rebuilds. code: scenario   (plain construction-time object; carries seed, partition_by)        │        ├──▶ data_shards  ( 2 rows ; partition cols dataset,sim,replace)        │         │        │         ▼  tar_map(values = data_shards, names = c(dataset, sim, replace))        │     data_step_*  ──▶ results/data/dataset=boron/sim=1/replace=FALSE/part.parquet  (2 named shard targets)        │        ├──▶ fit_shards   ( 4 rows ; partition cols dataset,sim,rescale; data upstream)        │         │        │         ▼  tar_map(values = fit_shards, names = c(dataset, sim, rescale))        │     fit_step_*   ──▶ results/fit/dataset=boron/sim=1/rescale=FALSE/part.parquet   (4 named shard targets)        │                  each shard reads the matching data shard by partition path        │        └──▶ hc_shards    ( 2 rows ; partition cols dataset,sim; fit upstream)                  │                  ▼  tar_map(values = hc_shards, names = c(dataset, sim))              hc_step_*  ──▶ results/hc/dataset=boron/sim=1/part.parquet                  (2 named shard targets, 4 tasks each)                          each shard reads the matching fit shard(s) by partition path     summary  ──▶ results/summary.parquet                 (reads all three layers via duckplyr) library(targets) library(tarchetypes)  # The scenario is a plain object built HERE, at sourcing time — not a # tar_target. Everything downstream is a pure function of it, so the # shard set is fixed before any target runs (static branching). scenario <- ssd_scenario(   ssddata::ccme_boron,   nsim = 2L,   nrow = c(5L, 10L),   rescale = c(FALSE, TRUE),   est_method = c("arithmetic", "multi"),   nboot = 10,   seed = 42L )  # One row per shard, computed now (sourcing time). Each row carries the # step's partition_by values (which become the tar_map target-name # suffix and the Hive path) plus a list-column `tasks` of the task rows # that shard must run. data_shards <- ssd_scenario_data_shards(scenario)  # 2 rows fit_shards  <- ssd_scenario_fit_shards(scenario)   # 4 rows hc_shards   <- ssd_scenario_hc_shards(scenario)    # 2 rows (4 tasks each)  list(   # tar_map mints one named target per shard at sourcing time. Independent   # shard targets run in parallel; how they pack into Slurm jobs under   # crew_controller_slurm() is an open question (§11).   #   # error = "null" so one bad shard does not abort the run: the target   # goes NULL, the error is recorded, the rest keeps building and summary   # unions the survivors (§6.2). format = "file" passes the shard path,   # not its value, between targets (see below).   tar_map(     values = data_shards, names = c(dataset, sim, replace),     tar_target(       data_step,       ssd_run_data_step(tasks, scenario, out_dir = "results/data"),       format = "file", error = "null"     )   ),    tar_map(     values = fit_shards, names = c(dataset, sim, rescale),     tar_target(       fit_step,       ssd_run_fit_step(tasks, scenario,                        data_dir = "results/data",                        out_dir  = "results/fit"),       format = "file", error = "null"     )   ),    tar_map(     values = hc_shards, names = c(dataset, sim),     tar_target(       hc_step,       ssd_run_hc_step(tasks, scenario,                       fit_dir = "results/fit",                       out_dir = "results/hc"),       format = "file", error = "null"     )   ),    tar_target(     summary,     ssd_summarize(dir_data = "results/data",                   dir_fit  = "results/fit",                   dir_hc   = "results/hc",                   path     = "results/summary.parquet"),     format = "file"   ) ) # `tasks` is the shard row's list-column of task rows (one per task in # this shard), supplied by tar_map from fit_shards. ssd_run_fit_step <- function(tasks, scenario, data_dir, out_dir) {   rows <- purrr::pmap_dfr(tasks, function(task_id, primer, data_id,                                           rescale, min_pmix, ...) {     upstream <- arrow::read_parquet(file.path(data_dir,                                               # partition path from upstream cols                                               .upstream_path(...),                                               "part.parquet"))     local_dqrng_state(scenario$seed, primer)     tibble::tibble(task_id = task_id,                    rescale = rescale,                    min_pmix = min_pmix,                    fit = list(fit_dists_state(upstream, rescale, min_pmix, ...)))   })   out <- file.path(out_dir,                    .partition_path(tasks, scenario$partition_by$fit),                    "part.parquet")   dir.create(dirname(out), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)   arrow::write_parquet(rows, out)   out } duckplyr::read_parquet_duckdb("results/data/**/*.parquet") |>   dplyr::filter(dataset == "boron", sim == 1L) |> dplyr::collect() duckplyr::read_parquet_duckdb("results/fit/**/*.parquet")  |> ... duckplyr::read_parquet_duckdb("results/hc/**/*.parquet")   |> ...

code: 1. Identify the failing task row.       ──────────────────────────────       # Find the task that errored within the failing branch:       # branch error message names the shard's partition path;       # within the shard the task_id of the bad row is in the log.       task <- tar_read(hc_tasks) |> dplyr::filter(task_id == "3ab9c7")        The row carries:         - primer    (length-2 integer = c(hi31, lo31), §2)         - all hc params (nboot, est_method, ci_method, ...)         - upstream fit-shard partition values (dataset, sim, rescale)           — enough to reconstruct results/fit/dataset=.../sim=.../rescale=.../part.parquet     2. Locate the upstream shard by partition path.       ────────────────────────────────────────────       results/fit/dataset=<...>/sim=<...>/rescale=<...>/part.parquet       The immediate upstream is enough; no need to walk further.     3. Sync to local.       ──────────────       rsync cluster:_targets/objects/hc_tasks   ./_targets/objects/       rsync cluster:<upstream_path>             ./<upstream_path>     4. Reproduce, no targets involved.       ───────────────────────────────       fit <- arrow::read_parquet(<upstream_path>) |>              # the fit shard may contain multiple fit task rows;              # pick the one this hc task points to.              dplyr::filter(task_id == task$fit_id) |>              dplyr::pull(fit) |> .subset2(1L)       options(error = recover)        # or place browser() in hc_state       out <- ssdsims:::hc_state(         data       = fit,         seed       = scenario$seed,         state      = task$primer[[1]],         nboot      = task$nboot,         est_method = task$est_method,         ci_method  = task$ci_method,         proportion = scenario$hc$proportion,         ci         = scenario$hc$ci,         parametric = task$parametric,         save_to    = NULL       )

what: 8.1 Path-axis growth — new shards, existing shards untouched
title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: Adding value axis partition_by step creates new partition cells therefore new shards; existing shards’ Parquets stay byte-identical reused. bookkeeping needed beyond targets’ usual branch-level dependency tracking.

title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: Adding value axis partition_by step changes task-row set every shard step’s partition tree (shard now one row’s worth tasks). affected shard atomically rewritten new task set: targets sees grouped task table change, marks branches stale, body re-runs K tasks shard overwrites Parquet. Per-task RNG identity preserved (task’s primer unchanged), rows come byte-identical previous Parquet — file new. -place append: one Parquet per shard, rewritten whole. Trade-accepted (Q2 design dialogue): simpler cache semantics exchange redoing work every task already shard. Whether summary rebuilds depends bytes, rewrite. independent experiment confirmed targets propagates value, act rebuilding: recomputed target whose value byte-identical leaves dependents skipped. shard gain row, Parquet bytes change summary (reads shard) re-runs. corollary matters §8.4 -op rewrite: rewrite reproduced exact bytes, format = "file" content-hashing see change summary rebuild. Byte-stable Parquet writes (pinned arrow, deterministic column order) make hash comparison meaningful. avoid rewrite cost, move axis partition_by step. trade shard count vs. cache reuse: pushing axis path means future growth axis adds new shards instead rewriting old ones, cost producing (smaller) Parquets front.

title: Targets design — ssdsims

(tar_cue(depend = FALSE) pins dependency/code changes ; target still rebuilds format = "file" output goes missing task-table grouping changes — makes path-axis inner-axis growth §8.1–§8.2 still work pin place.)

what: 8.4 Forced re-run after a code fix
title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: dual case: bug hc_state() fixed; user wants buggy shards rewritten patched code. Partition paths unchanged (fix doesn’t alter partition_by columns), file existence still says “cache hit”. Easiest move: targets::tar_invalidate(names = failed_branch_names) bookkeeping-preserving alternative — end state, call overrides §8.3 pin shards want refreshed. Either way patched code produces different bytes, rewritten shards’ content hashes change summary rebuilds (value-propagation, §8.2); shards left alone stay cached.

title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: Per-scenario manifest (small JSON sidecar results directory): seed — scenario’s RNG root (§2). datasets — name vector referenced tasks (§1.1). min_pmix — name vector ditto. fit, hc — argument-vector grids. partition_by — per-shard path axes (§5). completed_shards — set shard partition paths whose Parquet exists trusted, shard’s sha256 (recorded write time, including cloud copy’s sha256 upload set; see §6.1, §7). r_version, dqrng_version, ssdtools_version — versions pinned bit-stability across re-runs (§9). Restart property dqrng ((seed, state) ⇒ draw sequence) verified scripts/experiment-dqrng-hash.R.

what: dists and nboot are not fit/hc grid axes
title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: dists controls distributions ssdtools::ssd_fit_dists() fits given data slice; nboot controls many bootstrap iterations ssdtools::ssd_hc() runs. iterations live inside ssdtools exposed ssdsims level. Adding distribution dists therefore invalidates every fit branch (hash changes); raising nboot invalidates every hc branch. Reusing partial results — nboot = 100 ⊂ nboot = 1000 — require either () wrapping per-distribution / per-bootstrap iteration ssdsims dqrng stream aggregating, (b) ssdtools change expose inner loops. Sketch ; scope.

title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: internal RNG consumption ssdtools::ssd_fit_dists() ssd_hc() black box. install known (seed, state) call, many uniforms ssdtools call draws order ssdtools implementation detail. breaking change order future ssdtools release silently change bit-stable results even scenario seed unchanged. Mitigation: pin ssdtools (dqrng, R) versions scenario manifest.

title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: §5’s “nrow never axis” property — slice_sample_state returns prefix smaller n, replace values — holds long base::sample.int() byte-stable across R versions. Validated current R scripts/experiment-subset-property.R; pin R version (dqrng, ssdtools) manifest (§8.5) guard future behavior changes.

what: dqrng::register_methods() is scoped per scenario execution
title: Targets design — ssdsims
text: scenario execution (via ssd_run_scenario() cluster step) installs dqrng base R RNG backend entry restores exit via .exit(restore_methods()). Tests helper scripts touch methods mid-session (inside scenario runner) use .exit() discipline — documented CLAUDE.md (§RNG discipline).

Document assumption manifest pin R versions, implement sample.int-equivalent function explicit contract? Dataset identity. Datasets keyed name registry, digest::digest(df). Two registrations name different bytes silently collide. registry refuse re-registration unless byte-identical, carry content hash task ID alongside name? Force re-run inside one targets project. §8.4 lists two options re-running code fix: unlink() bad shards, tar_invalidate(names = ...). recommended path? unlink simpler loses bookkeeping; tar_invalidate bookkeeping-preserving cluster-aware (targets versions don’t propagate across remote workers). Manifest format. scenario manifest stores seed, datasets min_pmix name lists, fit hc argument-vector grids, upload spec, pinned package versions (§9). text: data ssd_data() collection (preferred), - convenience - single data frame (named unnamed) list data frames. Bare inputs validated via Conc contract ssd_data(). nsim count number data sets generate. seed scalar whole number; scenario's RNG root. Required - changing fully re-roots scenario's random-number draws. ... Unused; must empty. name optional dataset name single-data-frame form, overriding derived name. Must combined named list ssd_data() collection. nrow positive whole number minimum number non-missing rows. dists character vector distribution names. rescale flag specifying whether leave values unchanged (FALSE) rescale concentration values dividing geometric mean minimum maximum positive finite values (TRUE) string specifying whether leave values unchanged ("") rescale concentration values dividing geometric mean minimum maximum positive finite values ("geomean") logistically transform ("odds"). computable flag specifying whether return fits numerically computable standard errors. at_boundary_ok flag specifying whether model one parameters boundary considered converged (default = TRUE). min_pmix min_pmix function(s), referenced name. text: preferred form ssd_data() collection, owns validation naming: ssd_define_scenario(ssd_data(boron = ccme_boron, cadmium = ccme_cadmium), ...). convenience, bare data frame input also accepted four forms (routed Conc validation): single data frame, name derived argument expression: ssd_define_scenario(ssddata::ccme_boron, ...) gives "ccme_boron". single data frame explicit name=: ssd_define_scenario(ssddata::ccme_boron, name = "boron", ...). named list, names taken list: ssd_define_scenario(list(boron = ccme_boron, cadmium = ccme_cadmium), ...). unnamed list, names derived per element: ssd_define_scenario(list(ccme_boron, ccme_cadmium), ...).
